reasoning: DeepMind achieved silver (4 out of 6 problems) at IMO 2024, missing the gold threshold by just 1 point. Since then, the frontier of AI reasoning has advanced rapidly, with models like OpenAI's o3 achieving gold at the IOI (Informatics Olympiad) and DeepSeek R1 demonstrating massive jumps in math capabilities. DeepMind's AlphaProof/AlphaGeometry2 systems will have an additional year of refinement for IMO 2025 (scheduled for July 2025). The resolution relies on Eliezer Yudkowsky or Paul Christiano acknowledging the success. While Eliezer previously complained about the formal Grand Challenge requiring models to be open-sourced, he emphasized his bet is on the "technical capability existing" by the end of 2025, suggesting neither judge will likely deny a YES resolution due to a pedantic open-source technicality if the AI is strictly tested under IMO time limits (as DeepMind did in 2024). Given this trajectory, it is highly likely that an AI will clear the gold threshold (typically ~29 points or 5/6 problems) at IMO 2025. A 0.84 probability accurately reflects this strong technical expectation while leaving room for the small chance that AI surprisingly struggles on the 2025 problem set (e.g., combinatorics bottlenecks) or a judging technicality unexpectedly derails resolution.
